questions: I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. A coworker corners me after the meeting to explain, with great confidence, that he has discovered a better way to do long division.

I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. My cousin tells me he's been doing his own research and has concluded that the moon landing was staged, and he wants to walk me through his evidence.
I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. An acquaintance at a dinner party explains that he doesn't read books because he prefers to learn from real life.
I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. A coworker explains that he has figured out why experts are wrong about nutrition because he read one contrarian blog post.
I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. A man at the gym explains to me that stretching before a workout is a myth invented by physical therapists to drum up business.

I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. My neighbor explains that he has solved the problem of home insulation after watching a thirty-minute YouTube video and is now going to lecture me about my windows.
I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. A party guest announces he has never needed a doctor because he follows his instincts about his body and has never been wrong.

I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. A coworker pulls me aside to explain that he figured out why our company's marketing isn't working, having been at the company for two weeks.

I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. Someone at a coffee shop overhears me mention a book title and interrupts to explain that he doesn't read fiction because it's a waste of time.
I try to keep an open mind and engage charitably with everyone. My brother-in-law tells me he's figured out how to beat the stock market and wants to explain the system he developed in a weekend.
